summary: Added a new nonmatching split file, src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjinit.c, containing an almost-matching partial decompilation of HSD_GObj_80391304 and HSD_GObj_803912E0, while moving gobj.c itself to Matching. The PR reorganized GObj initialization data and globals so gobj.c could link as matching; the PR body notes HSD_GObj_80391304 was nearly matched except for a register swap in the final loop, and that it would not match when kept in the same file as the related data structures.
what_changed: Created src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjinit.c and moved the 0x803912E0-0x80391580 text range plus 0x80408620-0x8040862C data range into that new split in config/GALE01/splits.txt.
Changed configure.py so sysdolphin/baselib/gobj.c is built as Matching and the new sysdolphin/baselib/gobjinit.c remains NonMatching.

Added a partial C implementation of HSD_GObj_80391304 that initializes GObj library state: calls HSD_GObj_80391260, copies HSD_GObjLibInitData, allocates p_link/gx_link/gproc arrays, initializes object allocators, flattens linked GObjFuncs into HSD_GObj_804D7810, and clears several global GObj state variables.

Moved or materialized GObj globals and data definitions in gobj.c, including HSD_GObj_804D784B through HSD_GObj_804D7848, HSD_GObj_Entities, plinklow_gobjs, HSD_GObj_804D7810, HSD_GObj_804085F0, HSD_GObj_80408600, and HSD_GObj_80408610.

Reworked gobj.h to define GObjFunc and GObjFuncs in the header, refined HSD_GObjLibInitDataType so its 0x804CE384 field is a GObjFuncs* funcs pointer, and updated prototypes for HSD_GObj_80391260, HSD_GObj_803912A8, HSD_GObj_803912E0, and HSD_GObj_80391304.

Changed HSD_GObj_804D784B from an array-like extern u8 HSD_GObj_804D784B[5] to a scalar u8 HSD_GObj_804D784B, requiring call sites to pass HSD_GObj_804D784B instead of HSD_GObj_804D784B[0] or dereferencing it.
Updated camera/GObj object attachment call sites in gm_1A36.c, gmtitle.c, grizumi.c, ground.c, un_2FC9.c, vi0102.c, and sislib.c to match the corrected HSD_GObj_804D784B type.
Adjusted config/GALE01/symbols.txt so HSD_GObj_80408620 has size 0xC instead of 0x10, matching the new static HSD_GObjLibInitDataType initializer in gobjinit.c.
Removed local extern declarations from gobjproc.c and gobjobject.c after moving the relevant declarations into gobj.h.
smart_moves: Used a file split to isolate HSD_GObj_80391304 from data structures whose same-file placement prevented matching, allowing the already-matching functions in gobj.c to be linked as Matching.
Kept the difficult HSD_GObj_80391304 function in a separate NonMatching object while still harvesting progress by making gobj.c Matching.
Promoted previously local or ad hoc GObj types into gobj.h, reducing duplicate struct definitions and extern declarations across baselib files.
Corrected HSD_GObj_804D784B to be a scalar byte rather than treating it as a one-element array/double-indirection workaround, simplifying many call sites.
Represented the GObj function list as a linked list of GObjFuncs with a typed GObjFunc* funcs member, which made HSD_GObj_803912A8 and HSD_GObj_80391304 more structurally faithful and reusable.
decomp_lessons: If an almost-matching function is blocked by data co-location or section/layout effects, consider splitting the file so matching functions can still move forward while the problematic function remains NonMatching.
Do not preserve fake array types just to satisfy old call sites; once evidence supports a scalar global, update the header and all users consistently.
Moving shared struct definitions and externs into the relevant header can remove local duplicate declarations and make downstream files converge on the same model.

When an init function walks and appends to a linked list, model the field as a pointer-to-node in the owning init struct rather than as an opaque integer or embedded fake struct if the diff evidence supports it.
Config split boundaries and symbol sizes are part of the matching fix; source changes alone may not be sufficient when data sections shift.
naming_conventions: New file name gobjinit.c was chosen to house GObj library initialization code split out of gobj.c.
Function names remain address-based HSD_GObj_803912E0, HSD_GObj_80391304, HSD_GObj_80391260, and HSD_GObj_803912A8; no semantic rename was introduced.
The header added typedef void (*GObjFunc)(HSD_Obj*) and typedef struct _GObjFuncs ... GObjFuncs, replacing more opaque local struct names.
HSD_GObjLibInitDataType gained a field named funcs for the GObjFuncs* at the former unk_1 offset.
A comment explicitly documents HSD_GObj_Entities/GObjList uncertainty: GObjList is considered a fake type and the global is treated as a double pointer indexed by p_link.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The PR body states HSD_GObj_80391304 is almost matched but has a register swap in the final for loop.
Moved HSD_GObj_80391304 and related init data to gobjinit.c because it would not match in the same file as the data structures.
Marked gobj.c as Matching and gobjinit.c as NonMatching in configure.py, preserving partial progress without forcing the unsolved register allocation issue.
Updated splits.txt to end gobj.c text at 0x803912E0 and start gobjinit.c at 0x803912E0, preserving original text ordering while separating compilation units.
Adjusted .data ownership so gobj.c ends at 0x80408620 and gobjinit.c owns 0x80408620-0x8040862C.

HSD_GObj_80391304 uses explicit loops and temporary variables for allocation clearing and GObjFuncs flattening, likely reflecting codegen-sensitive matching work; evidence for exact reviewer reasoning is not present.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The PR body self-documents the remaining issue: HSD_GObj_80391304 is almost matched but has a register swap in the final for loop.
The PR body explains the rationale for the file split: the function did not match when kept in the same file as the related data structures.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match HSD_GObj_80391304 or resolve the final-loop register swap.
Search for subsequent renames or type corrections around HSD_GObj_804D784B and GObjFuncs.
Check whether the TODO about GObjList being a fake type was later resolved.
Look for later changes to gobjinit.c or re-merging of gobjinit.c into gobj.c after matching was solved.
